introduction: The marriage registration process in Tamil Nadu is centrally managed by the Registration Department through the TNREGINET portal under the Tamil Nadu Registration of Marriages Act, 2009.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Processing Time: Certificate is normally issued on the same day as the physical registering appointment.
  • Application Fee: Standard registration fee under Hindu Marriage Act is ₹100. Late registration (after 90 days) carries a penalty.
  • A Marriage Certificate is valid for a lifetime.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Marriage Registration

Note: You need to provide documents for Proof of Age, Proof of Address, Photographs, and Witnesses.
Proof of Identity & Age
  • Birth Certificate
  • Transfer Certificate (TC)
  • Passport
Proof of Address
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Passport
  • Ration Card
Evidence of Marriage & Photographs
  • Temple receipt / Church certificate
  • Wedding invitation
  • 4 passport-size photographs of both
Witnesses
  • Three witnesses who attended the marriage
  • Witness Aadhaar / Voter IDs
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via TNREGINET Portal

Tamil Nadu's TNREGINET portal (Inspector General of Registration) enables online pre-registration of marriages and appointment booking with the jurisdictional Sub-Registrar Office (SRO).

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit TNREGINET Portal
    Go to tnreginet.gov.in. This is the official Tamil Nadu Registration Department portal.
  2. Register / Login
    Click "User Registration". Enter your name, mobile number, and email ID, then verify via OTP. Log in with your credentials.
  3. Navigate to Marriage Registration
    Go to E-Services → "Marriage Registration". Select the applicable Act: Tamil Nadu Registration of Marriages Act, 2009 or Hindu Marriage Act.
  4. Fill Application Form (Form I)
    Enter complete details of the husband, wife, parents, date and place of marriage, religion, and full details of 3 witnesses including their address and Aadhaar numbers.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies (JPG/PDF, max 300 KB each): Birth Certificate/SSLC marksheet, Aadhaar, ration card, wedding photo, ceremony photo, invitation card, and 3 witness ID proofs.
  6. Book SRO Slot & Pay
    Select your jurisdictional SRO and book an appointment slot. Pay ₹100 online via the payment gateway. Download the Acknowledgement Receipt.
  7. Visit SRO on Appointment Date
    Both spouses and all 3 witnesses must appear at the SRO. Biometrics are captured. All parties sign the marriage register. Certificate is issued same day.
Download Certificate: After SRO registration, download the digitally signed certificate from TNREGINET using your application reference number.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Sub-Registrar Office (SRO)

Visit the Sub-Registrar Office (SRO) under whose jurisdiction the marriage was solemnized or where either spouse has resided for at least 30 days. Working hours: Monday – Friday, 10:00 AM – 5:45 PM.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Locate the Correct SRO
    Find the jurisdictional SRO at tnreginet.gov.in → 'Office Locator'. You may register in the SRO of the area where the marriage ceremony took place.
  2. Collect Form I
    Obtain the Marriage Registration Form I from the SRO counter free of charge. It is available in English and Tamil.
  3. Fill the Form Carefully
    Enter: husband's and wife's full names (as per SSLC), date and place of marriage, religion, names and addresses of 3 witnesses, and residential address of both spouses.
  4. Affix Photographs
    Paste one recent joint wedding photograph and one individual passport-size photograph of each spouse on the form. Have all photographs signed across.
  5. Attach Self-Attested Copies
    Attach: SSLC Certificate (age proof), Aadhaar, Ration Card (address proof), wedding invitation, and Aadhaar/ID proofs of all 3 witnesses. Bring originals.
  6. Submit, Sign & Pay
    Submit the form at the SRO counter. Both spouses and all witnesses sign in the presence of the Sub-Registrar. Pay ₹100 at the cash counter and collect the Receipt.
  7. Certificate Issued Same Day
    After biometric capture and signing, the Marriage Certificate is issued same day. It can also be downloaded from TNREGINET post-registration.
Helpline: Tamil Nadu Registration Dept: 044-25672700. For late registration (after 90 days) a penalty applies — approach the District Registrar for approval.
afterApplying:
After Registration - What Next?

Attendance & Signature

Physical attendance of husband, wife, and the three witnesses is mandatory at the Sub-Registrar Office for biometrics and signing the register.

Certificate Access

The digitized certificate will be available to download through the TNREGINET portal soon after successful SRO verification.
